# Copilot Lessons

Lessons learned during development of cli-intelligence.

## 2026-04-17

- **Myers' diff algorithm for better diffs**: Implement O(ND) Longest Common Subsequence algorithm instead of simple line-by-line comparison. Myers' algorithm finds shortest edit script (minimal changes), produces cleaner unified diffs with proper context grouping. Backtrack through trace arrays to reconstruct operations (Equal/Delete/Insert). Group into hunks with configurable context lines (typically 3).
- **Atomic multi-file transactions**: For coordinated edits across multiple files, use two-phase commit: (1) Create backups of all files, (2) Apply all edits. If any operation fails, rollback all changes from backups. Prevents partial failures that leave codebase in broken state. Track new files separately (delete on rollback, not restore).
- **Transaction isolation pattern**: Only allow one active transaction at a time. Starting new transaction automatically rolls back uncommitted previous transaction. Simplifies state management and prevents conflicting changes. Use `IsTransactionActive` flag to guard all transaction operations.
- **NuGet package management via CLI**: Prefer `dotnet add/remove package` commands over manual XML manipulation of `.csproj` files. CLI commands handle version resolution, dependency conflicts, and maintain proper XML formatting. Fall back to XML parsing only for read-only inspection (DotNetInspectTool).
- **Process output capture with async events**: For long-running processes (build/test/run), use `OutputDataReceived` and `ErrorDataReceived` events with `BeginOutputReadLine()` instead of `ReadToEnd()`. Prevents deadlocks when output buffers fill up. Use `StringBuilder` to accumulate output asynchronously.
- **Diff hunk grouping logic**: When too many consecutive Equal operations appear (> contextLines * 2), close current hunk and start new one. Add trailing context to old hunk and leading context to new hunk. Produces readable diffs that don't show entire unchanged files.
- **Safe file editing pattern**: For agent-driven file modifications, default to `dry_run=true` with unified diff preview. LLM sees changes before applying, can verify correctness, then explicitly set `dry_run=false` in second invocation. Always create backups (`.bak` files) before overwriting.
- **Unique target requirement**: When implementing replace operations in file editing tools, reject targets that appear multiple times. Force users to provide enough context for unambiguous matches. Prevents accidental multi-replacements that could break code.
- **Structured build diagnostics**: Parse MSBuild output with regex to extract `file(line,col): severity code: message` tuples. Return structured lists (not raw text) so agent loop can provide precise error locations to LLM for correction.
- **Agent validation loop**: Read → Inspect → Edit (dry-run) → Edit (apply) → Build → Iterate pattern enables self-correcting agents. Build diagnostics feed back as new user message, LLM sees errors with file/line/column, can call `apply_patch` again to fix. Up to 3 iterations prevents infinite loops.
- **Tuple deconstruction in pattern matching**: When switch expressions return tuples, use `var (success, message, content) = operation switch { ... }` instead of assigning to `var result` and accessing `.Success`/`.Message` properties. Avoids CS1061 errors about missing members on tuple types.
- **GeneratedRegex in .NET 10**: Source-generated regex with `[GeneratedRegex]` requires `partial` class and `partial` method. For simpler cases, use `new Regex(pattern, RegexOptions.Compiled)` field instead to avoid boilerplate.
- **Agent loop implementation**: Use `[TOOL: name param1=value1 param2="quoted value"]` format for LLM tool invocation. Parse with regex (`^\[TOOL:\s*(?<toolName>...) (?<params>...)\]$` multiline), handle quoted parameters, iterate up to max rounds (e.g., 3), feed results back as new user message. Always include user approval gate for Risky/Destructive tools.
- **Tool parameter metadata**: Extend `ITool` with optional `GetParameters() → ToolParameter[]` (default empty for backward compat). Use `ToolParameter(name, type, required, description, defaultValue)` to generate rich prompts. Call `FormatForPrompt()` to render parameter schemas for LLM.
- **Tool risk classification**: Use `[ToolRisk(level)]` attribute with `ToolRiskLevel` enum (SafeReadOnly, SafeWrite, Risky, Destructive). Default implementation in `ITool.GetRiskLevel()` reads attribute via reflection, returns SafeReadOnly if missing.
- **Path sandboxing for file tools**: Validate all file paths against allowlist of workspace roots (`Environment.CurrentDirectory`, `data/`, `storage/`). Use `Path.GetFullPath()` to normalize, then `StartsWith()` to check. Reject traversal attempts before any file I/O.
- **Default interface methods** (.NET 8+): Can add methods to interfaces without breaking existing implementations. Use for optional metadata methods like `GetParameters()` and `GetRiskLevel()`. Keep default implementations simple and reflection-based.
- When adding parameter metadata to tools via multi_replace, always include the method signature `(IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string> parameters)` in the new string if the replacement removes it. Missing method signatures cause cascade syntax errors.
- Use `.NET 7+ RegexOptions.NonBacktracking` for processing arbitrary user input to prevent Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS). The NonBacktracking engine guarantees O(n) linear-time execution regardless of pattern complexity, eliminating catastrophic backtracking vulnerabilities.
- Patterns incompatible with NonBacktracking (backreferences `\1`, lookaheads `(?=...)`, lookbehinds `(?<=...)`) must be rejected at boot with clear error messages. Use fail-fast validation in a singleton registry service to prevent runtime ReDoS vulnerabilities.
- For high-performance regex-based extraction systems, parse configuration files once at boot and cache compiled `Regex` objects with `RegexOptions.Compiled` in a singleton registry. This eliminates repeated I/O and regex instantiation overhead.
- Reflection-based dynamic metadata binding (iterating `Match.Groups.Keys` to populate dictionaries) enables configuration-driven systems where adding new extraction rules requires zero code changes—just edit the configuration file.
- When replacing specialized extractor classes with a single generic extractor, keep extractors that have unique dependencies (like `ReminderExtractor` with `ReminderService`) or special logic (like `MandatoryContextExtractor`'s field-update behavior). Only consolidate extractors that differ purely in routing logic.
- Adding a `Dictionary<string, string> Metadata` property to DTOs enables dynamic capture group binding without breaking existing JSON serialization. Use `[JsonPropertyName("metadata")]` and initialize with `new()` for clean serialization.
- When centralizing AI calls behind a single service (`AiInteractionService`), keep `OpenRouterClient` untouched as the low-level HTTP transport and wrap it rather than modifying it. This keeps the blast radius minimal.
- The extraction pipeline temporarily swaps the model on `OpenRouterClient` (cheap model for extraction, then restores). This works but is not thread-safe — if concurrency is ever added, the client needs a model-per-call overload instead.
- Fire-and-forget `Task.Run` for background extraction is fine for a CLI app with sequential user interactions, but would need a proper queue/channel in a server scenario.
- JSON extraction from AI models sometimes comes wrapped in markdown code fences (```` ```json ... ``` ````). Always strip those before deserializing.
- Deduplication in extractors relies on `string.Contains` against the current file content — cheap and effective for small files, but would need indexing for large knowledge bases.
- Workspace storage files (under `storage/`) are the source of truth; runtime `data/` files are synced copies. Extractors must write to both.
- OpenRouter's `verbosity` parameter only needs to be sent when non-default; use `JsonIgnoreCondition.WhenWritingNull` with a nullable property and set it to `null` for the default value (`"medium"`) to keep payloads clean.
- Anthropic prompt caching via OpenRouter requires a top-level `cache_control` field (not per-message). OpenAI caching is fully automatic — no payload changes needed. Gate caching logic on `model.StartsWith("anthropic/")`.
- Externalizing system prompts into a markdown file with H2-keyed sections (`## base`, `## chat`, etc.) makes them editable without recompilation. Always provide a hardcoded fallback in case the file is missing.
- When adding optional JSON fields to an API request model, prefer `JsonIgnoreCondition.WhenWritingNull` over conditional serialization — it keeps the model declarative and avoids manual `if` checks during serialization.
- `LibraryImport` (source-generated P/Invoke in .NET 7+) requires `<AllowUnsafeBlocks>true</AllowUnsafeBlocks>` in the `.csproj` — the generated marshallers use unsafe code even if your own code doesn't.
- Clipboard access on Windows from an async console app: `System.Windows.Forms.Clipboard` requires `[STAThread]` which conflicts with async `Main`. Workaround: shell out to PowerShell (`Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Windows.Forms; [Clipboard]::SetImage(...)`) or use raw P/Invoke.
- `[SupportedOSPlatform("windows")]` on a method isn't enough to suppress CA1416 at the call site — the caller must also guard with `OperatingSystem.IsWindows()` (not `RuntimeInformation.IsOSPlatform`).
- When adding optional parameters to a widely-called method (like `PromptBuilder.BuildMessages`), put them at the end with defaults — all existing call sites continue to work without changes.
- YAML frontmatter parsing for `SKILL.md`: a simple single-line regex per key is sufficient for the AgentSkills spec; no need for a full YAML parser dependency.
- For high-value memory capture (like phone/project facts), combine deterministic regex extraction with AI extraction. Keep deterministic items at confidence `1.0` so they are not filtered out by confidence thresholds.
- Keeping deterministic patterns in a markdown catalog (`storage/system-regex.md`) with H2 section keys and fenced `regex` blocks lets you evolve low-cost extraction rules without recompiling. Always keep hardcoded fallback patterns in code in case the catalog is missing or malformed.
- Use `TimeOnly.TryParse` (available since .NET 6) instead of `DateTime.TryParseExact` with format arrays for time-of-day parsing. It handles HH:mm, h:mm tt, 3pm, 9:00 AM natively in one call. Use `DateTime.TryParse` for full ISO 8601 inputs that include a date.
- Do NOT name a namespace `DateTime` — it shadows `System.DateTime` everywhere in that file and its dependants. Use `Time` or another unambiguous name.
- To set Kestrel's listening URL in a `CreateSlimBuilder` app, use `app.Urls.Clear(); app.Urls.Add(url)` after `Build()`. `builder.WebHost.UseUrls` is not available on the slim builder's `ConfigureWebHostBuilder`.
- `WebApplication.CreateSlimBuilder` gives the smallest ASP.NET Core footprint (no MVC/auth/CORS). Use it for embedded servers inside console apps.
- `ILoggingBuilder.ClearProviders()` requires `using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging` — not pulled in automatically by `CreateSlimBuilder`.
- Add `<FrameworkReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.App" />` (not a NuGet package) to bring Kestrel + Minimal API into a self-contained console exe. The framework reference is trimmed correctly with `PublishSingleFile`.
- `ReminderService` stores entries as JSON in `data/reminders/reminders.json`. Checked at the top of each chat loop iteration — no background thread needed for a sequential CLI app.

## 2026-06-12

- `LibraryImport` source-generated P/Invoke requires the **entire containing type chain** to be `partial` — both the inner `NativeMethods` class AND the outer tool class. Without `partial` on the outer class, SYSLIB1050/CS8795 errors fire.
- When using `[SupportedOSPlatform("windows")]`, applying it at the **class level** is cleaner than annotating individual methods — all internal calls are automatically satisfied, and external callers get a single clear signal.
- For SSRF protection in HTTP tools, resolve DNS first and check the IP against block lists (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, loopback, link-local, IPv6 ULA) **before** making the request. Don't rely on hostname alone — DNS rebinding can bypass hostname checks.
- When building a tool invocation loop: cap iterations (3 rounds), require user approval per tool call, strip `[TOOL: ...]` markers from displayed text, and feed tool results back as a system-role message so the model can incorporate them.
- DuckDuckGo Instant Answer API (`api.duckduckgo.com/?q=...&format=json`) works without an API key and returns `Abstract`, `Answer`, and `RelatedTopics` — useful for a zero-config web search tool.
- Read-only tool design (filesystem, git) limits blast radius — whitelist allowed actions rather than blacklisting dangerous ones.
- For environment variable display, redact values whose names contain KEY, SECRET, TOKEN, PASSWORD, CREDENTIAL, or API_KEY — prevents accidental secret exposure through system info tools.
- PowerShell script adapter safety: preview script content + parameters before execution, detect destructive patterns (Remove-Item, rm, del, Drop-Database, etc.) for double confirmation with `defaultValue: false`.
